summary: Found 7 material issues in the new MCP collaboration path. The most serious are a likely MCP framing break, an evaluation format mismatch that bypasses the existing rebuttal pipeline, incomplete turn-token redaction, and missing turn-lock enforcement on state transitions.

findings.title: Python MCP bridge switched to NDJSON framing, which is incompatible with the stdio MCP framing used elsewhere in this repo
findings.why_it_matters: `src/adapters/mcp-adapter.js` now connects through the official Node MCP stdio transport, but `src/mcp/codex_review_mcp.py` no longer reads or writes `Content-Length` frames. That will prevent initialize/tool calls from being parsed correctly, so MCP-backed reviews can fail before the first request completes.
findings.fix_instructions: Restore `Content-Length` framing in `read_framed_message()` and `write_framed_message()` (or replace the handwritten transport with the official Python MCP stdio server implementation). Then add an end-to-end test that launches `src/mcp/codex_review_mcp.py` via `StdioClientTransport`, performs `initialize`, and successfully calls `run_codex_review`.

findings.title: MCP `hub_advance_session` persists before appending lifecycle events, so event history is lost after restart
findings.why_it_matters: `server.store.save(session)` runs before `collab_state_changed` / `session_resolved` / `session_closed` are appended. Live subscribers see those events, but the stored session does not contain them, so reloaded history diverges from what clients already observed.
findings.fix_instructions: Move `server.store.save(session)` to after all `session.addEvent(...)` calls in `hub_advance_session`, matching the corrected REST path. Add a regression test that advances a session, reloads it from `SessionStore`, and verifies the lifecycle events are present.

findings.title: Turn tokens are still exposed in status/list responses via the `turn` object
findings.why_it_matters: You redact `message.turnToken`, but `session.turn` is returned verbatim from `hub_get_status`, `hub_list_messages`, and the REST list/status endpoints. Any client that can poll those endpoints can read the active turn token and then post turn-gated messages or impersonate the lock owner.
findings.fix_instructions: Before serializing any session/list/status response, clone `session.turn` and set `token` to `undefined`/`null`. Apply the same redaction in `src/mcp-server.js`, `src/mcp-collab-tools.js`, `src/collab-routes.js`, and any REST `GET /api/sessions/:id` response that includes `session.toJSON()`. Add tests that assert tokens never appear outside the direct `claim-turn` response.

findings.title: `hub_evaluate_findings` bypasses the existing rebuttal normalization pipeline and stores incompatible verdict values
findings.why_it_matters: The MCP tool accepts `accepted/rejected/disputed` and pushes those raw objects straight into `session.rebuttals`/`session.evaluations`. The rest of the app expects normalized rebuttals with targets and verdicts like `accept/reject/defer/needs_evidence`. Rerun prompt generation and appeal outcome derivation will operate on malformed data or silently skip it.
findings.fix_instructions: Make `hub_evaluate_findings` call the same normalization/upsert logic used by `apiEvaluateFindings` in `src/rebuttal-routes.js` and `src/hub/rebuttal.js`. Reject evaluations unless the session is completed, map MCP verdicts onto the canonical internal verdicts, and persist normalized rebuttals instead of ad-hoc objects.

findings.title: Fresh sessions stay in `draft` even though default reviewer/responder assignments already exist
findings.why_it_matters: `Session` initializes `assignments` but leaves `collabState` as `draft`, and no creation path calls `initCollab()`. A brand-new review therefore cannot be claimed immediately through the collaboration tools even though the default assignments are already populated.
findings.fix_instructions: Initialize `collabState` from `transitionOnAssignments(this.assignments)` during construction, or call `initCollab()` from every session creation/retry path (`apiCreateSession`, MCP `hub_create_review`, and rerun creation). Add a test that a newly created session starts in `awaiting_codex_turn` and can be claimed without a separate assignment step.

findings.title: Expired turns can be reclaimed from non-waiting states and incorrectly rewind `awaiting_resolution` back into review
findings.why_it_matters: `claimTurn()` allows a claim whenever `turn.status === 'expired'`, even if `collabState` is not an awaiting-turn state. If a turn expires while the session is in `awaiting_resolution`, reclaiming it drives the state through `claimStateForAgent()` and can turn resolution back into `antigravity_reviewing`.
findings.fix_instructions: Restrict `claimTurn()` to explicit waiting states only. When a turn expires, clear the turn metadata and keep the collab state stable, or add dedicated decider-turn handling instead of reusing reviewer/responder review states. Add tests for expiry in `awaiting_resolution` and in active review states.

findings.title: State transitions are not protected by the turn token, so the turn lock can be bypassed
findings.why_it_matters: `advanceCollabState()` validates only `agentId` and current state. The REST/MCP advance endpoints do not accept a `turnToken`, so any caller that knows the expected agent ID can complete a review, request a rerun, or release a turn without actually owning the claim.
findings.fix_instructions: Add `turnToken` to the REST and MCP advance APIs and require `ensureTurnOwner()` for turn-sensitive actions (`review_complete`, `request_response`, `request_rerun`, `release_turn`, and any other action that should be serialized by the active turn). Add tests proving stale or parallel callers are rejected without the matching token.

fix_plan: Restore MCP stdio framing compatibility and cover it with a real Node-client-to-Python-server handshake test.
Route MCP finding evaluation through the same rebuttal normalization/state checks as the REST API.
Redact `session.turn.token` from every non-claim response, including MCP status/list and REST session/list endpoints.
Move MCP advance-session persistence to after lifecycle events are appended, and verify stored history after reload.
Initialize collaboration state on session creation and tighten turn semantics for expiry and advance actions with token-validated tests.
